mnemonics.title: IPC Functions Memory Aid
mnemonics.content:
Message Queues: msgGET creates, msgSND sends, msgRCV receives, msgCTL controls
Shared Memory: shmGET creates, shmATtaches, shmDTaches, shmCTL controls
Semaphores: semGET creates, semOP operates, semCTL controls

Pattern: GET-CREATES, OP/CONTROLS, SEND/RECEIVE, ATTACH/DETACH, CTL-CONTROLS

mnemonics.title: ftok() Memory Trick
mnemonics.content:
ftok = File TO Key
ftok(pathname, project_id) -> unique key
Same path + same proj_id = same key across processes

acronyms.usage: Generates IPC key from file path and project ID
Flag to create IPC facility if not exists

visualTips.visualization: Think of a message queue as a FIFO tube where messages (envelopes) are dropped in and taken out. Each message has a type label (mtype) for sorting.
Imagine a whiteboard hung between two rooms. Both processes can see and write on it directly - fastest communication but need to coordinate who writes when.
Think of a semaphore as a counting device at a resource (like parking slots). P operation (wait) decrements count, V (signal) increments. When count is 0, must wait.
Like generating a unique locker combination (key) using room number (path) and person ID (proj_id) - same inputs give same combination.

analogies.analogy: Like a postal service - you send letters (messages) with addresses (mtype), they wait in the post office until recipient collects them. Asynchronous delivery.
Like a shared whiteboard in an office - multiple people can read/write simultaneously but need to coordinate (that's why we need semaphores). Fastest but requires synchronization.
Like a restaurant reservation system - counting available tables. When table is taken (P/wait), count decreases. When guest leaves (V/signal), count increases. Prevents overbooking.

analogies.anal analogy: Pipes are like walkie-talkies (byte stream, one-way), Message Queues are like emails with subject lines, Shared Memory is like a shared document, Semaphores are like traffic signals.
